completed_tasks.name: Architecture note and root-package/capability-plane convergence map
Root-package and directory convergence into domain-owned packages
Capability Plane v2 definitions, availability modeling, and /capabilities
Ownership tests, capability-plane tests, and full verification

decisions.decision: Use a bounded root-directory convergence slice for low-coupling helpers instead of forcing broad package-namespace churn in one pass.
decisions.rationale: This reduced the root directory materially while keeping the runtime stable and preserving scope for Capability Plane v2.

decisions.decision: Capability Plane v2 is the canonical substrate layer, with /capabilities as the primary capability-centric view.
decisions.rationale: This removes the need for /commands and /state to each carry the full capability burden alone.

decisions.decision: /commands remains route-centric, /state remains runtime-centric, and both align to the same capability definitions and availability model used by /capabilities.
decisions.rationale: This preserves layered exposure without endpoint sprawl or semantic drift.

next_action: Resume by deciding whether to continue with post-phase branch work on feat-1.4.0-interface-evolution-mainline or plan the next roadmap step. If continuing execution, start from the completed Phase 25.1 results rather than re-running the phase.
context_notes: Phase 25.1 is complete and verified. The branch contains the full architecture-convergence pass: root-directory convergence for low-coupling helpers, Capability Plane v2, a new GET /capabilities route, and alignment across /commands, /state, and /capabilities. The worktree is clean. The only open work is strategic follow-up after this completed phase, not unfinished implementation.
